subject: defconfig: update.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: exFAT filesystem volume dirty flag synchronization is lazy instead of eager; mitigates potential data-loss scenarios in power-fail during file operations on SD card

notes: CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC is a Samsung exfat driver feature that does not exist in vanilla 6.18 Kconfig. This commit (on MiSTer-v5.14 branch only) toggles it from disabled to enabled in the fork's v5.15 defconfig. Vanilla 6.18's exfat driver lacks this option entirely. Our 6.18 linux.config does not include CONFIG_EXFAT_DELAYED_SYNC even though stock-linux.config has it enabled=y; this is expected since our mainline exfat lacks the feature. The Samsung exfat symlinks are carried via patch 0031-exfat-samsung-symlinks.patch, but that patch does not include the DELAYED_SYNC codepaths (which were part of the fork's Samsung driver implementation). No userspace code references this option. Disposition: dropped-deliberate — the fork feature was not forward-ported to 6.18's mainline exfat.
